title: The visual politics of trauma and gatekeeping in Indian family courts
description: For litigants in Indian courts, justice rarely arrives as a guaranteed package. It is more often a trail of breadcrumbs that may or may not lead to the sought-after redress.This uncertainty becomes further treacherous in the wood-panelled corridors of family courts, where a quiet but pervasive trial often takes place long before any evidence is weighed – the trial of the survivor’s demeanour.Against this backdrop, it is important to understand why the family courts were established.
